message:
fp-frame: add to_json "table" orient with Table Schema metadata

Implements pandas' `to_json(orient='table')` output, which wraps DataFrame
data in a Table Schema envelope that standalone viewers (JupyterLab,
pandas-native readers, vega-lite, etc.) consume directly. Prior to this
commit only the primitive orient values (split, records, index, columns,
values) were supported — callers requesting `table` got
CompatibilityRejected.

Core additions:

- dtype_to_table_schema_type(): mapping from internal DType to Table
  Schema type strings (boolean, integer, number, string, duration, any).
  Categorical dtypes are serialized as "string" to match pandas.
- index_label_to_table_schema_type() / index_labels_to_table_schema_type():
  produce the index's "type" field. For a single-label index we pick
  the label's native type; for mixed-type labels we fall back to
  "string" (pandas behavior).
- New "table" branch in DataFrame::to_json that builds a schema object
  (fields[], primaryKey) alongside the row data array. The schema's
  `fields` list includes the DataFrame's own columns plus the index
  name, with types driven by the dtype_to_table_schema_type mapping.
  The orient refuses to serialize if the index name collides with an
  existing column label (same rejection pandas raises) and the error
  text matches pandas' message format for that collision case.

Keeps the existing orient branches unchanged and preserves the
DataFrameDictResult / to_dict split introduced in the previous commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
